page: Amazon Web Services m8i.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and is supported by a large 480 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying on network-attached storage, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a strong performance profile in single-threaded operations. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench text processing and strong ratings in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core metrics also demonstrate competitive performance. However, multi-core and multi-threaded execution present a clear tradeoff; stress-ng multi-core performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Passmark database operations fall into the weaker performance categories. Memory bandwidth for large blocks remains average, while small-block cached memory bandwidth is relatively weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i.xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or heavy multi-threaded parallelization. The inclusion of advanced Intel instruction sets, such as Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512, enhances its capability for lightweight vector and matrix operations, including small-scale LLM inference. This instance is best suited for workloads such as static web serving, single-threaded text and data processing, software compilation, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance includes a substantial 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. It lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on network storage and offering a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show a strong capability in single-threaded tasks, such as text processing and compression, contrasted with weaker multi-core and database performance. This resource profile makes the m8i.xlarge a qualitatively cost-effective choice for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks rather than highly parallelized database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon CPU at 3.9 GHz, and 16 GB of DDR5 RAM. It delivers strong single-core performance for web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, and strong single-threaded performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and strong single-core processing capabilities for diverse workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 7 GHz
Strong single-threaded performance in text processing and compression
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
EBS-only storage configuration with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose applications
